channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Late-April snowstorm in Saskatchewan leads to dozens of crashes and hazardous driving conditions
item.description: A late-season snowstorm swept through parts of Saskatchewan, particularly Saskatoon, creating dangerous driving conditions and leading to dozens of collisions. Strong winds combined with heavy snowfal...

item.title: WA Vietnam Veteran Reflects on Conscription and Its Long-Term Impact
item.description: John McPherson, a 78-year-old Vietnam War veteran, shared his experience of being conscripted at the age of 20, reflecting on the impact it had on his life and the lives of others. Initially told he w...

item.title: Authorities clamp down on dangerous pavement driving outside Johannesburg school
item.description: Parents and motorists in Johannesburg have been urged to follow road rules after a viral video showed vehicles driving on pavements outside Roosevelt High School in Roosevelt Park during busy morning ...

item.title: Nation honours Anzac Day with dawn services across Australia
item.description: Across Australia, Australians observed the solemn tradition of Anzac Day this morning, gathering at Dawn Services in towns and cities nationwide. This day serves as a moment to remember and honour the...

item.title: Manitoba Considers Ending Seasonal Clock Changes by 2026
item.description: Manitoba Premier Wab Kinew has indicated that the province is moving closer to ending the practice of changing clocks twice a year, potentially by the end of 2026. The decision would involve choosing ...

item.title: Metro Vancouver to implement early Stage 2 water restrictions amid drought concerns
item.description: Metro Vancouver will implement Stage 2 water restrictions starting May 1, skipping the usual Stage 1 measures due to unusually dry conditions and concerns about drought. Officials say this early escal...

item.title: Guide to Joining Bloom Membership Global for BINI World Tour Presale Access
item.description: The article explains how fans can gain early access to ticket presales for the BINI Signals World Tour 2026 by joining the Bloom Membership Global. This membership is facilitated through Weverse, a po...

item.title: International expert panel launched to guide countries in phasing out fossil fuels
item.description: A new international panel of scientists, economists and energy experts has been established to support countries in reducing their reliance on fossil fuels while addressing associated risks such as vo...

item.title: Virginia Giuffre's family requests meeting with King Charles during US visit to support abuse survivors
item.description: The family of Virginia Giuffre has renewed calls for King Charles to meet with them during his upcoming state visit to the United States, aiming to show support for survivors of Jeffrey Epstein’s abus...

item.title: Japan Enhances Defence with New Missiles Amid Rising Tensions in East Asia
item.description: Japan is embarking on a significant shift in its military strategy, deploying new long-range missile systems on the southern island of Kyushu. These missiles, developed domestically by Mitsubishi Heav...

item.title: Netanyahu Reveals Prostate Cancer Treatment and Full Recovery
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu recently shared with the public that he was diagnosed with prostate cancer but has now fully recovered after treatment. The disclosure came in a personal stat...

item.title: Audi F1 names Allan McNish as racing director ahead of Miami Grand Prix
item.description: Audi’s Formula One team has confirmed the appointment of Allan McNish as its new racing director, with the former driver set to take up the role from the upcoming Miami Grand Prix. The announcement ma...

item.title: Filipino Cuisine Gradually Gains Ground in Japan Through Community Roots and Modern Dining Concepts
item.description: Ipinapakita ng artikulo ang unti-unting pag-usbong ng Filipino food sa Japan, kahit matagal na may malaking Filipino community doon. Noong early 2000s, mahirap makahanap ng pagkaing Pinoy at kadalasan...

item.title: Tinubu Swears in Dr. Muttaqha Darma as New Housing Minister
item.description: President Bola Ahmed Tinubu on Friday officially swear in Engr. Dr. Muttaqha Rabe Darma as the new Minister of Housing and Urban Development, following the resignation of the former minister, Ahmed Mu...

item.title: Peace Talks Underway: US and Iran Meet in Pakistan
item.description: Pakistan is currently hosting the second phase of peace talks between the United States and Iran, a significant development amidst ongoing tensions in the Middle East. The motorcade believed to be car...

item.title: Late-April Snowfall Hits Saskatchewan Despite Earlier Warm Weather
item.description: Saskatchewan is experiencing unpredictable weather this spring, with a sudden return to snow after a period of unseasonably warm temperatures. While residents had begun enjoying golf and outdoor activ...

item.title: Prosecution Pushes for Transparency on Duterte’s ICC Legal Team Status
item.description: The prosecution in the ongoing International Criminal Court (ICC) case involving former President Rodrigo Duterte has requested that the status of Duterte's legal team be made public. This move comes ...

item.title: Orange County deputies shoot suspect after domestic dispute escalates
item.description: Early on Friday morning, Orange County sheriff’s deputies shot a man in his 20s in Lake Forest following a domestic disturbance call. The incident began around 2:30 a.m. at a home in Rancho Mission Vi...

item.title: Aboriginal Veterans' Trauma, Activism, and the Fight for Self-Determination
item.description: This article delves into the often-overlooked experiences of Aboriginal Australians during and after World War I. It highlights the trauma faced by veterans like Louis Lacey, who struggled with the af...

item.title: Meta Expands AI Infrastructure with AWS Graviton Chips to Support Agentic Systems
item.description: Meta has announced a new partnership with Amazon Web Services (AWS) to integrate tens of millions of AWS Graviton CPU cores into its computing infrastructure. This move is aimed at supporting the comp...
